ops.title: cc-transcript resolves from PyPI (>=13.1,<14); the sibling checkout is v14 WIP
ops.body: cc-steer pins `cc-transcript>=13.1,<14` from PyPI with NO `[tool.uv.sources]` editable override (dropped in 3a0b56e, completing the intent of the empty cc66ac9). The `../cc-transcript` checkout carries unreleased v14 WIP — frozen native views (events not constructible from Python, so `dataclasses.replace` dies), `WatchTailer` replacing `TailState`, `preview_schema` on `ContextWindow` — with a stale `13.0.0` version string. Released 13.1.0 keeps the API `daemon.py` and `live.py` are written against. Re-adding the editable source silently substitutes that WIP for the pin (uv ignores specifiers on path sources) and breaks the suite. Adopting v14 is a real migration: it needs a cc-transcript release plus an upstream scrub/rewrite primitive (per-event text scrubbing has no idiomatic form on frozen views). The `tinker<0.23` cap in the retrain extra is deliberate: an unattended weekly job bumps a fast-moving SDK on purpose, not by drift. cc-steer-lab still carries its own editable cc-transcript source — repoint it the same way once the in-flight E12 run finishes (do not `uv sync` the lab mid-run).
